title: Missing Airman Raises Concerns That Iran Could Gain Leverage Over the U.S.
summary: Since 1979, Iran has repeatedly used Americans and Europeans detained on its territory to win concessions over more powerful adversaries.

title: What We Know About the F-15E Strike Eagle Shot Down by Iran
summary: An F-15E Strike Eagle was shot down over Iran and an A-10 Warthog crashed near the Strait of Hormuz, officials said on Friday. Two airmen were rescued, and one is unaccounted for.

title: Trump Pledged a Quick End to the Iran War, but He Hasn’t Explained How
summary: President Trump bet that American firepower could cow Iran into compliance. So far, Iran’s leaders have been unwilling to quit.

title: New Attorney General, Same Albatross: Trump’s Quest for Retribution
summary: The name atop the Justice Department’s organizational chart matters less than the presence of a president whose demands for revenge have become so extreme that even his most obsequious appointees have fallen short.

title: Trump Contemplates Other Cabinet Changes as He Faces Political Clock
summary: With the midterms approaching, the president may be running out of time to get new cabinet members confirmed without bipartisan support.

title: Trump Budget Does Little to Address Nation’s Fiscal Challenges
summary: While the federal deficit has started to shrink under President Trump, his plans could make the long-term situation worse.

title: Epstein Presented Himself to Indian Tycoon as a Trump White House Insider
summary: The convicted sex offender gave Anil Ambani information on appointments and foreign policy. Some seemed prescient, though there was no evidence he was close to the administration.

title: Trump Wants to Make Deportation Deals. Autocrats Are Ready to Listen.
summary: The White House has turned deportations, a signature domestic issue, into a major piece of foreign policy. Here’s what we know about the program.

title: Foreign Doctors Forced Out of U.S. Hospitals by Trump Immigration Policy
summary: Physicians from 39 countries are being pushed out of U.S. hospitals as a policy blocks their ability to work.

title: ICE Review Finds Dozens of Violations at Texas Migrant Detention Center
summary: A review of Camp East Montana in Texas found failures in medical care, disease control and oversight, including improperly documented uses of force.

title: Houston Cheers on Artemis II Moon Mission, Reclaiming Its Place as ‘Space City’
summary: The Artemis II mission elicited deep feelings for many Americans, particularly in Houston, the home of mission control.

title: ‘Hacks’ Has the Last Laugh
summary: As the HBO comedy finished production earlier this year, a photographer captured some of the final days on set and the showrunners looked back on its award-winning run.

title: Pam and Kristi, Kicked to the Curb
summary: Trump girls gone wild — or just gone.

title: Trump’s Relentless, ‘Utterly Incoherent’ Battles
summary: From Tehran to the Supreme Court, a look at Trump’s relentless battles.

title: Living Space
summary: We don’t have to travel to the moon to gain some of the insight that astronauts do.

title: Fight Escalates Over Plan to Demolish and Rebuild N.Y.C. Public Housing
summary: The city thinks four developments in Chelsea are too run-down to be saved, and wants to rebuild them, adding mixed-income housing. Some residents are opposed.

title: Three Charged With Arson in Attack on U.K. Jewish Charity’s Ambulances
summary: Two men and a teenager were arrested this week, the London police said, adding that the March attack had not been declared a terrorist incident.

title: After 11 Years in Court, Heir Reclaims a Modigliani Looted by the Nazis
summary: A judge ruled against a holding company controlled by David Nahmad, the billionaire art dealer, which had bought the work at auction in 1996.

title: She Wanted Her Hair Done for a Special Day. She Left the Salon in Tears.
summary: A Black woman is pushing for changes in the hair industry after claiming in a lawsuit that she and her 7-year-old were turned away from an Ulta Beauty salon in Manhattan because of their hair texture.
